video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 15
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, July 15th: Crews search for three missing after boat sinks off San Francisco; Iran threatens to cut off all Middle East energy exports; ICE ordered to suspend most vehicle stops; House votes to make Daylight Saving Time permanent.

video:video.video:title: Survivors and victims' relatives of deadly Bangkok bar fire seek compensation, gather belongings
video:video.video:description: The cause of the fire at the Rong Beer Na Ladprao bar is still under investigation. More than 70 people were injured and 30 people remain hospitalized, with 15 of them in intensive care units, according to Bangkok's Erawan emergency services center.

video:video.video:title: 'The Odyssey' stars bring epic to New York
video:video.video:description: Director Christopher Nolan gathers his cast for the New York premiere of his take on "The Odyssey," starring Matt Damon as Odysseus, Tom Holland as his son and Zendaya as the goddess Athena.

video:video.video:title: At least 3 people killed following Russian airstrike on Sumy
video:video.video:description: Russian forces dropped six powerful glide bombs mostly targeting infrastructure in Ukraine’s northern Sumy region, killing three people and wounding seven, the head of the regional military administration Oleh Hryhorov said.

video:video.video:title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
video:video.video:description: Proponents, including the White House, argued the change would provide more daylight during the times that Americans are most active.

video:video.video:title: Lupita Nyong'o: 'Homer would be very proud'
video:video.video:description: Why has "The Odyssey" endured for centuries? At the film's New York premiere, Lupita Nyong'o said it's the power of the story that has kept the epic alive.

video:video.video:title: 1 dead and 3 missing after boat carrying mostly family members sinks near Alcatraz Island
video:video.video:description: One person died, three people were missing and 16 were rescued from the waters off San Francisco after a pontoon boat sank Tuesday afternoon while carrying mostly family members as part of a memorial service, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike overnight kills parents and their daughter in central Gaza
video:video.video:description: Mourners prayed and grieved for three Palestinians from the same family who were killed by an Israeli airstrike overnight into Wednesday, health authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Fuel oil found on beach in Russia’s port city of Taganrog on the Sea of Azov
video:video.video:description: Fuel oil was found on a beach in Russia’s port city of Taganrog on the Sea of Azov, authorities said on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer answers questions from lawmakers for the last time as PM
video:video.video:description: UK PM Keir Starmer, who leaves office next week, bid farewell to the boisterous weekly Prime Minister’s Questions sessions where he has traded barbs with opposition politicians and defended his government’s record.

video:video.video:title: France’s parliament set to approve bill allowing medically assisted dying
video:video.video:description: France’s National Assembly is set to give final approval Wednesday to a bill allowing adults with incurable illnesses to receive lethal medication, the culmination of years of debate over end-of-life care.

video:video.video:title: ICE should keep making traffic stops despite recent shootings, Trump says
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says Immigration and Customs Enforcement should continue vehicle stops after recent fatal shootings, seeming to oppose a new suspension of the practice used as part of his immigration crackdown.

video:video.video:title: Argentine directors discuss Maradona's infamous 'Hand of God'
video:video.video:description: Was it a hand ball? Was it a “magic trick?” Argentine directors of “The Match,” Juan Cabral and Santiago Franco, discuss Maradona’s “hand of God” from the 1986 World Cup quarter-final between Argentina and England.

video:video.video:title: Heatstroke alert issued for Tokyo as temperatures soar
video:video.video:description: According to the Tokyo Fire Department, 48 people were taken to hospital for heatstroke by later afternoon on Tuesday, with patients ranging in age from early 20s to mid-90s.

video:video.video:title: Spain and Gibraltar remove border fence in historic EU-UK treaty
video:video.video:description: The physical border between Gibraltar and Spain disappeared at midnight between Tuesday and Wednesday following years of post-Brexit wrangling over the contested territory of 40,000 people at the tip of the Iberian Peninsula.

video:video.video:title: Alex Cameron talks lyricism, words 'that aren't allowed' ahead of new album
video:video.video:description: Ahead of the release of his new album, "Late to Set," out July 24, Alex Cameron spoke with The Associated Press about poetry and words "that aren't allowed."

video:video.video:title: A 12-year-old baseball prodigy trains through tears after Venezuela quakes leave him homeless
video:video.video:description: At what was once his baseball field, 12-year-old Yeferson Seijas now lives with his family in a makeshift tent after twin earthquakes devastated Venezuela's coastal state of La Guaira.

video:video.video:title: Heavy rains cause flooding and mudslides in Russia's Sochi
video:video.video:description: Videos posted Wednesday on social media by local residents showed streets covered with mud and debris after torrential rain and thunderstorms caused the Bzugu River to overflow its banks.

video:video.video:title: Tiny golden statues of Mozart celebrate his 270th birthday
video:video.video:description: Three hundred golden statues of Mozart have been installed at the Mozarteum Foundation in Salzburg. The 50-centimetre tall figures mark the composer's 270th birthday - but some have already been stolen.

video:video.video:title: Iran releases video of missile launches as US military says it strikes Greater Tunb Island
video:video.video:description: Iran's military released video of missile launches as back-and-forth strikes between Tehran and Washington continued. U.S.

video:video.video:title: Memorial site grows for Maine motorist fatally shot by ICE
video:video.video:description: Community members stopped to leave flowers, signs and candles at a growing memorial for Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, a 25-year-old Colombian national fatally shot by ICE.

video:video.video:title: Face-to-face with ancient Rome: Exhibition in Hungary brings forgotten faces to life
video:video.video:description: A peasant girl, a slave and a soldier from an ancient Roman city in modern Hungary have been given faces and imagined lives nearly 2,000 years after death.

video:video.video:title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz appears before Senate committee
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration's latest nominee to lead the nation' top public health agency drew frustrated reactions from some U.S. senators on Wednesday when they pressed her on whether she would protect the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention from political meddling.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Democrats grill Trump's pick for Director of National Intelligence on his role in elections
video:video.video:description: Senate Democrats aggressively questioned President Donald Trump's pick for a key intelligence post on how he would approach the issue of election interference ahead of the fall elections.

video:video.video:title: Federal government installs reworked panels about slavery at George Washington's Philadelphia home
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration has replaced an exhibit on slavery at the site of President George Washington’s home in Philadelphia with a version that historians say whitewashes the nation’s history.

video:video.video:title: Alex Cameron jokes about growing anxiety over artificial intelligence
video:video.video:description: Ahead of the release of his upcoming album, "Late to Set," Australian recording artist Alex Cameron spoke with The Associated Press about growing anxiety over artificial intelligence and how it informs his music.

video:video.video:title: Anthony Michael Hall: 'I owe such a debt of gratitude to John Hughes'
video:video.video:description: The actor who is celebrating over 50 years in showbusiness credits the late filmmaker for much of his success.

video:video.video:title: Why a traffic cone got a hero’s welcome in Boston
video:video.video:description: A traffic cone arrived in Boston on a first-class flight, complete with bagpipes and an official welcome.

video:video.video:title: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche defends handling of Epstein files
video:video.video:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche defended the Justice Department's handling of the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files in his Senate confirmation hearing Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 15
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, July 15th: US reimposes blockade, increases strikes as Iran threatens to halt energy exports.; Blanche faces questions in Senate confirmation hearing; wildfire smoke will engulf swaths of US; golden Mozart statues mark his 270th birthday.

video:video.video:title: Tom Holland talks working alongside 'The Odyssey' cast
video:video.video:description: Tom Holland says "The Odyssey" sets held "a treasure trove of lessons" for him and Zendaya.

video:video.video:title: Anthony Michael Hall reflects on 50-year career
video:video.video:description: Anthony Michael Hall, known as a member of the iconic 80s "Brat Pack," found fame in films like "Sixteen Candles," "The Breakfast Club" and "National Lampoon's Vacation."

video:video.video:title: Coast Guard plans to suspend search at sunset after boat sinks near Alcatraz
video:video.video:description: U.S. Coast Guard crews searched a massive area for three people missing nearly a day after a boat carrying 20 family members and friends during a memorial service capsized in cold, choppy waters near Alcatraz Island, authorities said Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Rita Ora on playing Queen of Hearts in 'Descendants'
video:video.video:description: Rita Ora says she connected with Brandy "on another level" while making "Descendants: Wicked Wonderland."

video:video.video:title: Olivia Dean dedicates song to immigrant families during concert
video:video.video:description: At a Los Angeles concert, Olivia Dean dedicates “Carmen" — about her grandmother's immigration journey — to immigrant families.

video:video.video:title: Former ship captain pleads guilty to all charges related to 2019 drugging and assault of student
video:video.video:description: John Merrone plead guilt to all five counts of an indictment charging him with aggravated sexual abuse, sexual abuse, and abusive sexual contact relating to the rape of a 21-year-old United States Merchant Marine Academy student cadet.

video:video.video:title: Sabrina Bryan 'never stopped wearing cheetah'
video:video.video:description: Sabrina Bryan says cheetah print never left her wardrobe. She will return to her iconic Disney role as Dorinda in "The Cheetah Girls: Next Gen."

video:video.video:title: Living through sizzling temperatures in one of the world’s hottest towns
video:video.video:description: The northern Indian town of Banda has endured weeks of extreme heat, ranking among Asia’s hottest towns, with temperatures peaking at 48 degrees Celsius (118 degrees Fahrenheit).

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans celebrate World Cup win while England supporters are left 'gutted'
video:video.video:description: Argentina fans gathered in front of the Atlanta stadium to celebrate their semifinal win while England fans left disappointed. (AP Video by Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: NY Mayor Mamdani watches World Cup semifinal match with Rikers Island prisoners
video:video.video:description: New York City’s official World Cup guide lists no less than 100 free places to take in soccer’s biggest tournament. One place that isn’t listed?

video:video.video:title: France’s National Assembly gives final approval to assisted dying bill after years of debate
video:video.video:description: France’s National Assembly gave final approval Wednesday to a bill allowing adults with incurable illnesses to receive lethal medication, the culmination of years of debate over end-of-life care.

video:video.video:title: Three strikes hit different zones of central Gaza after warning from the Israeli army
video:video.video:description: Three strikes hit central Gaza on Wednesday evening after the Israeli military issued a warning, according to area residents.

video:video.video:title: Argentina reaches the World Cup final after 85 minutes of anguish in Buenos Aires and London
video:video.video:description: Football fans cried, sang and jumped in Buenos Aires and London on Wednesday after Argentina came from behind to defeat England 2-1 and reach the World Cup final for the second consecutive tournament.

video:video.video:title: Trump delays and rejects disaster aid for Democratic states at higher rates, AP finds
video:video.video:description: When major disasters strike, Americans are routinely waiting weeks — or even months — to receive presidential approval for aid.

video:video.video:title: Czech superfan dreams of Argentina glory in World Cup 2026
video:video.video:description: One Argentina super fan's three-story house, about 90 kilometers (56 miles) east of Prague, is painted in the iconic sky-blue and white colors and features a giant mural of the Argentine national team from the Qatar World Cup 2022, a life-size statue of Diego Maradona in the courtyard and a backyard

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 16
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, July 16th: U.S. intensifies strikes on Iran; Trump says ICE should keep making vehicle stops; Flooding in South Texas; Argentina going to World Cup finals.

video:video.video:title: At least two people killed and five injured after Russia's overnight attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: At least two people were killed and five injured, including a child, following Russia's overnight missile attack on Kyiv on Thursday, Ukraine's Emergency Service said in a statement on Telegram.

video:video.video:title: India's Gen Z 'cockroaches' took protest to the streets. Now they rally around a hunger strike
video:video.video:description: 59-year-old activist Sonam Wangchuk has become an unlikely symbol of India’s Cockroach Janta Party, a youth-led movement that erupted online two months ago and gained momentum over alleged leaks on social media in the country’s fiercely competitive college entrance exams.

video:video.video:title: Flooding forces evacuations in parts of South Texas
video:video.video:description: Heavy downpours drenching South Texas, causing floods and forcing evacuations. More than six million people in 57 counties were under a National Service flood watch, and in some areas flash flood warnings are in effect.

video:video.video:title: An empty middle seat is the latest perk United Airlines will offer on some flights
video:video.video:description: United Airlines is trying to attract flyers with another luxurious perk: an empty middle seat. They announced plans this week to roll out a new row of its “Economy Plus” seats with no neighbor in the middle spot — and instead use the space for a shared table

video:video.video:title: Protest in Kyiv against Zelenskyy's decision to dismiss Defence Minister Fedorov
video:video.video:description: Dozens of protesters gathered in central Kyiv on Thursday to denounce President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's decision to dismiss Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov after just six months in the post.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine buries its unknown soldiers as identification likely to go on for years
video:video.video:description: In a military cemetery in Kyiv, a brother and sister walk between the crosses, carrying a bunch of carnations.

video:video.video:title: UK urges FIFA to investigate Argentina over Falklands banner at World Cup
video:video.video:description: During post-match celebrations, Argentine players held a banner handed over by fans in the stands, reading “Las Malvinas son Argentinas” – “The Malvinas are Argentine.”

video:video.video:title: AP finds that Trump delays and rejects disaster aid for Democratic states at higher rates
video:video.video:description: When major disasters strike, Americans are routinely waiting weeks — or even months — to receive presidential approval for aid.

video:video.video:title: Joy in Oyo as rescued students are reunited with their relatives after months in captivity
video:video.video:description: Scenes of joy unfolded in Oyo, Nigeria, as rescued students reunited with their relatives after months in captivity.

video:video.video:title: At least two people killed and five injured after Russia's overnight attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: At least two people killed and five injured after Russia's overnight attack on Kyiv

video:video.video:title: Ice melting statues in front of the Colosseum to protest against climate change
video:video.video:description: Greenpeace Italy and Italian main labour union CGIL installed three ice statues, representing a farmer, a bicycle rider and a construction worker, and left them to melt under the scorching sun in front of the Colosseum on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: United by grief, mothers in Brazil demand reparations after police killed their sons
video:video.video:description: When a Brazilian police officer killed Ana Paula Oliveira’s 19-year-old son in a Rio de Janeiro favela in 2014, the mother of two didn’t think she would survive the grief.

video:video.video:title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
video:video.video:description: There will be no turning back the clock if the House has its way. The House passed a bill Tuesday that would make daylight saving time permanent

video:video.video:title: Former UK MP Ann Widdecombe killed in 'targeted attack' say police
video:video.video:description: Former politician and reality TV contestant Ann Widdecombe was killed in a “targeted attack,” though the motivation is still under investigation, British counterterror police said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Starmer chairs his last Cabinet meeting ahead of his last Prime Minister’s Questions
video:video.video:description: Cabinet ministers arrived at 10 Downing Street on Wednesday for the last meeting chaired by outgoing British Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: Movie lovers in Rome brave the extreme heat to watch their favourite films outdoors
video:video.video:description: Movie lovers in Rome are braving the extreme heat, armed with fans and water, to watch their favourite films outdoors against the backdrop of iconic monuments such as the Colosseum or the dome of St.

video:video.video:title: Heavy rains keep drenching South Texas, residents encouraged to seek higher ground
video:video.video:description: Homes in Boerne, Texas were surrounded by heavily flooded streets as city officials said they have shut down roads.

video:video.video:title: U.S. military releases video it says shows strikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it ended its latest wave of airstrikes targeting Iran. Iran retaliated with missile and drone fire targeting Bahrain and Kuwait before dawn.

video:video.video:title: Train crew in Canada surrounded by intense flames
video:video.video:description: Footage of a Canadian train crew surrounded by thick flames was posted to social media sites by Ontario Parliament member Sol Mamakwa. He said the crew members are safe.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire flames surround train in Canada
video:video.video:description: Ontario Parliament member Sol Mamakwa posted footage of thick wildfire flames surrounding a train crew in Canada to social media.

video:video.video:title: Texas flooding surges from huge rainstorms as rescuers pull people from rising waters
video:video.video:description: Floodwaters overran the city of Uvalde, Texas overnight, cutting off access to surrounding areas. Rescue crews rescued at least one child from floodwaters.

video:video.video:title: What a permanent switch to daylight saving time could mean for you
video:video.video:description: Daylight saving time is that period between spring and fall when clocks in most parts of the United States are set one hour ahead of standard time.

video:video.video:title: Settler violence surges across the West Bank
video:video.video:description: Settler violence surges across the West Bank

video:video.video:title: One Tech Tip: How to avoid texting thumb
video:video.video:description: The term texting thumb can describe a wide range of phone-related pains caused by tired-out tendons and achy joints.

video:video.video:title: Hailstorm pounds southern France
video:video.video:description: Hailstorm in Vals-les-Bains in southern France

video:video.video:title: Here's what to know about the science behind wildfires
video:video.video:description: Fire is a natural process that cleans up dead vegetation and releases nutrients back to forests. But experts say wildfires are becoming more severe, exceeding what forests can handle.

video:video.video:title: Korean-Australian alternative hip-hop collective 1300 on becoming a 'home' for each other
video:video.video:description: Korean-Australian alternative hip-hop collective 1300 talks about never feeling fully part of either world and deciding to become "a home" for each other through music.

video:video.video:title: UK urges FIFA to investigate Argentina over Falklands banner at World Cup
video:video.video:description: The British government on Thursday urged FIFA to investigate Argentina’s team after players posed with a banner claiming sovereignty over the contested Falkland Islands.

video:video.video:title: Texas flooding surges from huge rainstorms as rescuers pull people from rising waters
video:video.video:description: Dangerous flooding in Texas has intensified after days of pounding rain, sending emergency crews scrambling to rescue people from the rising waters before sunrise Thursday and setting off urgent warnings from forecasters.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfire in northeast Spain
video:video.video:description: Firefighters try to control a vicious wildfire in the north-eastern part of Spain.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire smoke blankets Toronto, raising air quality concerns
video:video.video:description: Smoke from wildfires blanketed Toronto on Wednesday, creating hazy skies and prompting health concerns as residents faced poor air quality, according to CTV.

video:video.video:title: Uvalde, Texas residents deal with flooded streets and neighborhoods
video:video.video:description: Residents in Texas assess flood waters in neighborhood streets, many impassable, after days of pounding rain.

video:video.video:title: Delve into the secret lying underneath Japan's ancient Asuka-Fujiwara sites
video:video.video:description: The ancient Japanese capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara have been submitted to UNESCO as one of the nominations for this year's new World Heritage Sites.

video:video.video:title: Trump fires new Seattle US attorney less than an hour after federal judges appoint him
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump fired the new top U.S. prosecutor in Seattle less than an hour after he was unanimously appointed by the federal judges in the district, highlighting tensions between the courts and the president over the powerful positions.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy shakes up government, drawing protests across Ukraine against ouster of defence minister
video:video.video:description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy shook up his wartime government, drawing thousands into the streets Thursday across Ukraine to protest the ouster of his youthful defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov.

video:video.video:title: Uvalde, Texas residents deal with flooded streets
video:video.video:description: Catastrophic flooding in Texas after days of pounding rain forced authorities to rescue dozens of people from rising waters before sunrise Thursday while many more fled to higher ground across a region still recovering from devastating floods just a year ago.

video:video.video:title: Ghost gun company ordered to pay $100M in the death of a Kentucky teen
video:video.video:description: A ghost gun company has been ordered to pay over $100 million in the death of a Kentucky teenager who had purchased the company's pistol-building kit online.

video:video.video:title: King Charles has a pint to 'drown a few sorrows' after England's World Cup loss to Argentina
video:video.video:description: King Charles III said "maybe it's a good day to drown a few sorrows" as he poured a pint of beer at a pub a day after England's World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina.

video:video.video:title: Keanu Reeves finds humanity behind the horsepower
video:video.video:description: While promoting ‘Hooligans: The ARCH Racing Project,’ Keanu Reeves said his racing documentaries aren't just about speed.

video:video.video:title: Slumping AI stocks drag down markets around the world
video:video.video:description: Drops for computer chipmakers and other AI winners dragged down stock markets worldwide Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Low testosterone testing explained
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday that he is rolling out a new screening program for “testosterone deficiency” among troops.

video:video.video:title: Trump's teleprompter operator on unpaid leave for alleged prediction market bets on Trump speeches
video:video.video:description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said she does not know of other White House employees accused of similar allegations, adding that the White House has “extremely strict, ethical guidelines with respect to issues like this.”

video:video.video:title: Resident struggles to get medicine for wife because of flooded roads in Uvalde, Texas
video:video.video:description: Uvalde resident Toby Guerrero says he's struggling to find medicine for his wife's vertigo because all the stores near him are closed as a result of catastrophic flooding in Central Texas.

video:video.video:title: Who is performing in the World Cup final halftime show?
video:video.video:description: The World Cup final will feature a star-studded halftime show for the first time. Here's who will perform.

video:video.video:title: Fighter jet flys low over a crowded beach in Florida
video:video.video:description: In the latest manuever, video spreading widely on social media shows a jet from the Navy's demonstration squadron, the Blue Angels, flying so low over a crowded beach in Pensacola on Wednesday that chairs and tents went flying and children held their hands over their ears.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 16
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, July 16th: Deadly flash flooding in Texas; Wildfires create hazardous air quality from Great Lakes to East Coast; Reports suggest White House teleprompter operator placed bets on Trump speeches; King Charles laments England's World Cup loss.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire smoke covers New York, Chicago
video:video.video:description: Wildfire smoke blankets New York, Chicago and much of the U.S. East Coast and Midwest triggering hazardous air quality warnings.

video:video.video:title: U.S. Navy fighter jet flies low over beach
video:video.video:description: Watch the moment a jet from the Navy’s Blue Angels flew so low over a crowded beach in Pensacola on Wednesday that chairs and tents went flying.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire smoke blankets Toronto
video:video.video:description: Smoke from wildfires blanketed Toronto on Wednesday, creating hazy skies and prompting health concerns as residents faced poor air quality, according to CTV.

video:video.video:title: Toy Insider showcases holiday toy trends at Sweet Suite
video:video.video:description: Toy companies are offering an early preview of toys expected to top holiday wish lists at the annual Sweet Suite event, which showcases the latest games, collectibles, and must-have releases.

video:video.video:title: Ex-wife of ICE officer in Maine shooting tells AP he has history of violent behavior
video:video.video:description: The Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, his ex-wife tells AP.

video:video.video:title: Ex-CEO of Italian highway operator and 31 others convicted in deadly 2018 bridge collapse
video:video.video:description: Dozens of family members of the victims packed the courtroom as the presiding judge read the verdicts against 57 defendants, including former executives and officials.

video:video.video:title: Keanu Reeves on starting a motorcycle company: It's David and Goliath!
video:video.video:description: Keanu Reeves and custom motorcycle builder Gard Hollinger are sharing the story behind their company, ARCH Motorcycle, in the new Samsung TV PLUS docuseries “Hooligans: The ARCH Racing Project.”

video:video.video:title: Marines conduct verification boarding on M/T Wen Yao in the Gulf of Oman, says US Central Command
video:video.video:description: American forces have redirected three commercial vessels attempting to breach the blockade, disabled one vessel that did not comply, and boarded another to ensure full adherence to the ongoing U.S. naval blockade against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Indigenous women in Bolivia take their traditional attire to skateboarding event
video:video.video:description: A group of Bolivian women and girls wore their traditional Indigenous attire in La Paz on Tuesday as they competed in a skateboarding youth talent show.

video:video.video:title: US ends its latest round of airstrikes on Iran, the sixth night in a row
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said early Friday that it had ended its latest round of airstrikes targeting Iran, the sixth night in a row of strikes on the Islamic Republic, as fighting over the Strait of Hormuz intensifies.

video:video.video:title: Japanese beetles threaten Italian vineyards
video:video.video:description: An invasion of Japanese beetles is threatening Italy's vineyards. Swarms of the pests are munching through leaves, leaving grapes exposed and putting this year's harvest in danger.

video:video.video:title: President Xi addresses an artificial intelligence conference in Shanghai
video:video.video:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping called for global cooperation on artificial intelligence, warning against countries placing their own national security interests above those of others.

video:video.video:title: A simple pair of glasses enhances productivity in some Bangladesh garment factories
video:video.video:description: In Bangladesh, home to the world’s second-largest garment industry after China, some factory owners are working on supplying more glasses to workers to boost productivity.

video:video.video:title: Trump uses primetime address to raise doubts about US elections ahead of midterms
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump used a primetime address to the nation Thursday to elevate his yearslong push to raise doubts about the legitimacy of U.S. elections and dispute his 2020 loss in an appeal for more restrictive voting laws ahead of the midterms.
